title: CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP) Stock Plunges 11% Following Dismal Earnings and Regeneron Competition
text: CRISPR Therapeutics Stock Reaction to Competition and Earnings Disappointment. Stock Performance: CRSP stock plunged 11% following earnings disappointment and news of competitive pressure from Regeneron's gene therapy announcement. The selloff reflected investor concerns about near-term commercial challenges and competitive threats. Earnings Disappointment: The company reported Q1 earnings that missed analyst expectations on revenue, reflecting slower-than-anticipated CASGEVY adoption rates. The revenue miss raised questions about the commercial trajectory of the company's flagship product. Regeneron Competition: Regeneron secured regulatory approval for Otarmeni, a competing gene therapy that it intends to provide at zero cost to qualifying U.S. patients. This no-cost strategy raised concerns about potential pricing pressure across the gene-editing industry. Market Implications: The announcement highlighted the competitive threats facing CRISPR, particularly around pricing strategy. If other competitors follow Regeneron's no-cost model, it could pressure industry-wide economics. Investor Reaction: The combination of earnings disappointment and competitive threats triggered a significant sell-off, with institutional investors reducing positions. Analyst sentiment turned more cautious, with some downgrading ratings. Recovery Outlook: Analysts noted that the fundamental story remained intact but that near-term execution risks and competitive dynamics needed to be carefully monitored.
